hints.boards: Kanban boards — daily work and columns.
hints.reports: Org-wide BI with charts and Explain (AI) on each metric.

hints.dashboard: C-level portfolio health, OKRs, anomalies, and throughput trend.

hints.tracer: Client-side error buffer (Flux diagnostics) for admins.

hints.platformAdminConsole: Organizations, all users, and audit log (platform admins only).
hints.sprints: Sprints across boards, lifecycle, and ceremonies.

hints.copilot: Board chat: summaries, search, and actions. On a board it opens the panel; elsewhere it goes to your boards list.

variant.toggleTooltip: Menu style: switch between Aurora (depth) and Clean (minimal).
variant.cycleTooltip: Menu style: {current}. Click to switch between Aurora and Clean.

theme.cycleTooltip: Appearance: {current}. Click to cycle system, light, and dark.

platformAdmin.title: Platform administration
platformAdmin.subtitle: Global view of organizations, users, and audit events.
platformAdmin.kvWarning: Without a persistent database (MongoDB) configured on the server, global listings are limited (dev mode). Ask your platform administrator to complete data configuration.
platformAdmin.passwordMin: Password must be at least 8 characters.
platformAdmin.deleteOrgConfirm: Delete {name} and all related data (users, boards, invites, etc.)? This cannot be undone.
platformAdmin.requestTimeout: The request took too long. Check your connection or try again.

sprints.title: Sprints
sprints.subtitle: Sprints from every board you can access.
sprints.upgradeRequired: Sprints are available on paid plans.
sprints.upgradeTitle: Sprint hub requires a paid plan
sprints.confirmDelete: Delete this sprint? This cannot be undone.
sprints.hubCadenceHintScrum: Sprint lifecycle, ceremonies, and the list below.
sprints.hubCadenceHintKanban: Run Kanban cadence ceremonies for a board (Business+).

backlog.title: Sprint items
backlog.hint: Commit unfinished cards. In an active sprint, changes affect scope and metrics.
backlog.readOnlyHint: Sprint in review or closed — scope cannot be edited here.
backlog.boardMismatch: Open this board to load cards and manage the backlog.
backlog.emptyCommitted: No cards in the sprint yet.

detail.emptyDone: No cards marked done for this sprint.
detail.noBurndown: Burndown unavailable (set start/end dates and scope cards).
detail.aiHint: Questions about scope, burndown, and changes recorded in sprint data.
detail.aiError: Could not get an AI answer.

aiSuggestions.q2: Which scope cards are still in progress?

detail.reconstructedHint: Card data is reconstructed from the current board; this sprint was closed before scope snapshots were stored.

history.subtitle: Timeline, velocity, and exports for this board.
history.closedCount: {count, plural, one {# closed sprint} other {# closed sprints}}
history.chartEmpty: Close a sprint to see velocity here.
history.hubBoardHistoryHint: Timeline and exports for the filtered board.

form.planningAiCreateHint: Save the sprint first to use AI planning suggestions on the board.
form.reviewSave: Confirm the data and click Save.

msg.scopeDriftHigh: High scope drift — renegotiate items before review.
msg.lowCommitment: Commitment below time pace — adjust plan or remove items.
msg.burningTooFast: Burning ahead of ideal — consider pulling prioritized items.
msg.runningOutOfTime: Sprint close to end with pending items — decide scope cut.
msg.earlyDays: Sprint starting — fix a daily cadence and review the goal.

timeline.title: Sprints timeline
timeline.subtitle: Velocity, deliveries and releases chained through history.

detail.title: Sprint detail
detail.reconstructedHint: Older than snapshot support — details reconstructed from the current board.

ceremonies.planningSubtitle: Capacity check, AI suggestions, and Monte Carlo hint.
ceremonies.standupSubtitle: What you did, what’s next, blockers — shared with the board.
ceremonies.overCommitted: More cards than rough capacity — consider dropping scope.
ceremonies.emptyStandup: No entries yet for this date.
ceremonies.scrumPlaybookBody: Definition of Ready: clear criteria before entering the sprint. Definition of Done: tested, shippable increment. Time-box planning, daily, review, and retro to keep rhythm. Use the backlog below for explicit sprint commitment.
ceremonies.planningBacklogIntro: Committed backlog — adjust scope before starting the sprint.

commandPalette.footer: ↑↓ navigate · Enter select · 1–9 quick · Esc close
commandPalette.aiComingSoon: Natural language (/ai) will connect here when Copilot function calling ships.
commandPalette.aiHint: Type a natural language command after /ai (e.g., 'move urgent cards to In Progress', 'open dashboard')

adminTracer.title: Tracer — diagnostics
adminTracer.subtitle: Read-only view of the same client buffer as the floating panel (?fluxDebug=1). Export JSON to share with engineering.
adminTracer.fluxDebugOff: off (events still accumulate from global handlers when the app runs)
adminTracer.selectPrompt: Select an entry on the left.

hotkeys.title: Keyboard shortcuts
hotkeys.subtitle: Press ? outside text fields to open this sheet.
hotkeys.escape: Close dialog or palette (when one is open)

rateLimitAbuse.title: Abuse — rate limit
rateLimitAbuse.subtitle: Grouped by IP or user when the global API limit returns 429 (Mongo with TTL).
rateLimitAbuse.empty: No blocks recorded in this period.

templates.title: Templates & showcase
templates.matrixSectionHint: A 4×4 grid with two level axes (like common prioritization matrices): drag board cards into cells. Publishing requires an administrator.
templates.bpmnSectionHint: Dedicated BPMN workspace with import/export in Markdown and BPMN XML.

matrixExclusive.title: 4x4 Matrix
matrixExclusive.heading: Exclusive 4x4 Matrix session
matrixExclusive.hint: Dedicated context to prioritize, create tasks, and publish templates with less visual noise.

bpmnExclusive.title: BPMN workspace
bpmnExclusive.heading: Exclusive BPMN session
bpmnExclusive.hint: Import, validate, edit and publish BPMN templates with focus on process modeling.

matrixPanel.hint: Pick a source board, then open the wizard to select cards, quadrants, and template metadata.
matrixPanel.noBoards: No boards available. Create a board before publishing.
matrixPanel.nonAdmin: Only administrators can publish templates to the showcase.
matrixPanel.nonAdminHint: You can still import matrix templates from the list below (Matrix badge) or ask an org admin to publish one.

matrixWorkspace.intro: Place cards on a 4×4 grid: vertical and horizontal axes represent level (low to high). The color gradient follows the rising priority diagonal, matching common market matrix patterns.
matrixWorkspace.bestPracticeHint: Best practice: map impact on the vertical axis and effort/complexity on the horizontal axis; high-impact and low-effort items usually get top priority.
matrixWorkspace.poolEmpty: All cards are on the grid or this board has no cards.
matrixWorkspace.dragHint: Drag from the list to a cell, between cells, or back to the list to remove from the grid.
matrixWorkspace.quickTaskTitle: Create a quick task for this matrix
matrixWorkspace.quickTaskHint: The task is created on the selected board and is immediately available to place in the matrix.
matrixWorkspace.deleteTaskConfirm: Delete this task from the board?
matrixWorkspace.taskGenericError: Could not save the task to the selected board.
matrixWorkspace.publishHint: Opens the wizard for title, category, and the current grid layout.

templates.subtitle: Import columns, labels, and automations without cards. Premium uses a 70/30 creator/platform split.
templates.aiHint: Answer a few questions — after each step we refresh a live board preview. Edit columns before creating.
templates.aiPlaceholder: e.g. B2B CS team running onboarding, QBRs, and renewals…

aiConv.intro: Four short questions (under two minutes). Your preview updates after each answer.
aiConv.q1: What kind of team is it?
aiConv.q2: How many stages does your process have? Describe briefly.
aiConv.q3: Which metrics matter most? (SLA, revenue, volume, quality…)
aiConv.q4: Any automation you want? (alerts, email, escalations — or “none”)
aiConv.ph2: e.g. 5 stages: triage → analysis → delivery → review → done
aiConv.ph3: e.g. SLA 24h, monthly revenue, ticket volume, NPS
aiConv.ph4: e.g. alert when card stalls 48h in “Waiting”
aiConv.ready: Preview is ready. Adjust columns on the right if needed, then create the board.
aiConv.publishAfter: After creating, open the flow to publish this structure to the showcase
aiConv.okrsTitle: Starter OKRs (created on the board when your plan includes Goals)

deleteTemplate.confirm: Do you want to delete this published template?

exportModal.title: Publish as template
exportModal.hint: Exports columns, automations, and labels (no cards). Visible in the showcase.
exportModal.hintMatrix: Eisenhower matrix as four columns; include copies of selected cards (no link to the source board after import).
exportModal.hintGrid4Publish: Set the template title and category. The 4×4 layout and card positions come from the matrix workspace.
exportModal.grid4Summary: {count, plural, one {# card on the grid} other {# cards on the grid}}
exportModal.revenueHint: Premium templates use a 70% creator / 30% platform split (commercial setup).

reports.title: Self-service BI with AI narrative
reports.subtitle: Live dashboard aggregating boards in your organization. Use Explain on each chart to generate an executive narrative.

hub.leadCycleNote: Lead time approximates creation → completion. Cycle time prefers column entry (`columnEnteredAt`) → completion when cards record moves.
hub.dorReadyHint: Non-done Scrum cards with all DoR checkboxes marked vs. cards that started filling DoR.
hub.forecastUnavailable: Forecast unavailable: no base board in heatmap.
hub.lssBlurb: DMAIC load, aging, completions, Pareto tags, and process capability (heuristic).

reports.emptyChart: Nothing to plot for this chart yet.
reports.copilotHint: Tip: weekly throughput and created vs. done use Copilot history on boards. Without MongoDB or Copilot usage, these may stay at zero.

cfdDaily.title: Cumulative flow
cfdDaily.emptyChart: No daily snapshots yet. Run the anomaly cron to record history.
cfdDaily.wipRisingBanner: Open WIP appears to be rising in this period — review work-in-progress limits and bottlenecks.
cfdDaily.sparseHistory: Fewer than 14 days with a recorded snapshot; missing days use the last known values.
cfdDaily.tooltipWipAlert: Alert: open WIP is trending up in this period.

cycleTime.title: Cycle time analysis
cycleTime.hint: Per completed card: completion date minus card creation (proxy for entering the flow). Column-to-column movement history is not stored; the tooltip shows the board’s configured flow and the column at completion. Percentiles (P50, P85, P95) follow the selected period and priority.
cycleTime.emptyChart: No completed cards with a completion date for the current filter.

hints.leadTime: Lead time for completed cards is estimated from card creation to last board update (approximation).
hints.heatmap: Color reflects risk exposure: red = more critical; green = healthier. Based on {appName} heuristics.
hints.velocity: Requires an owner (or assignee) field on cards; otherwise the chart may be empty.
hints.sentiment: Average 0–100 mood score per digest week from card text and Daily Insights (batch, no individual attribution). Data appears after the weekly digest job runs with MongoDB enabled.

lss.title: Executive DMAIC portfolio
lss.subtitle: C-level view of LSS boards: phase load, aging risk, completions, and OKR links.
lss.upgradeHint: This report requires a Business plan or higher. Upgrade to unlock Lean Six Sigma executive analytics.
lss.emptyNoLssBoards: No Lean Six Sigma boards in this organization yet.
lss.emptyNoLssBoardsHint: Create a board with methodology "Lean Six Sigma" (DMAIC) to see portfolio metrics here.

charts.tagPareto: Pareto — tags on open LSS work

hints.dmaicOpen: Non-completed cards counted by current column (bucket key).
hints.weeklyDone: Cards marked complete with a completion date in each rolling week.
hints.agingOpen: Days since card creation or column entry — distribution of all open items.
hints.tagPareto: How often each label appears on non-completed cards in LSS boards.
hints.individualsSpc: Last completed items: cycle days with control limits from moving range (educational view; not a full MSA/SPC program).
hints.capability: Cp/Cpk against a speculative USL = P95 × 1.25 of the same sample — for discussion, not contractual specs.

lss.paretoEmpty: No tags on open LSS items to rank.
lss.spcEmpty: Need a few more LSS completions with dates to draw limits.
lss.tollgateBody: Between phases, align with your sponsor on outputs: VOC & charter (Define), baseline & measurement plan (Measure), verified causes (Analyze), pilot & benefits (Improve), control plan & reaction plan (Control).
lss.sipocHint: Use the LSS assistant in Define to draft SIPOC, or store a SIPOC draft on the board when that field is enabled.
lss.portfolioHint: Sorted by at-risk WIP and maximum aging.

sprint.title: Predictive sprint planning
sprint.hint: Lightweight Monte Carlo (1000 runs): bootstrap of weekly Copilot throughput, or—if missing—empirical cycle times (column entered → done). Requires ≥4 week windows and either throughput or ≥8 completions with cycle time recorded.
sprint.tooltipExplain: Each bar is a percentile of the simulated weekly completion distribution (P50 = median; P70–P95 upper bands). The last column forecasts the next sprint; earlier columns use leave-one-out validation on history.
sprint.recommended: Suggested sprint composition (priority × due urgency × 1/expected cycle)

dependencies.title: Dependency map (cross-board)
dependencies.hint: Interactive D3 graph of links between cards. Adjust minimum confidence or switch board vs. org view.
dependencies.empty: No links match the current filters.
dependencies.needBoard: To filter by board, add ?boardId= when opening Reports or use the Org view.

proactive.title: Proactive AI
proactive.subtitle: Daily anomaly checks (throughput, WIP, lead time, OKRs, due dates) with z-scores vs. baseline. Alerts are stored here.
proactive.noMongo: Connect MongoDB to enable history and automatic alerts.
proactive.noMongoPublic: Proactive alert history isn’t available in this environment right now.
proactive.neverRunPublic: No automated checks have been recorded yet.
proactive.cronHintPublic: Automated checks run on a schedule configured for your workspace.
proactive.neverRun: No runs recorded yet. Check scheduling is configured by your platform administrator in the deployment environment.
proactive.cronHint: The daily job runs on the server with secrets defined only in the deployment environment. Weekly emails can include an optional summary of these alerts.

executiveDashboard.title: Real-time executive dashboard
executiveDashboard.subtitle: Portfolio health score, OKR progress, active anomaly alerts, throughput trend, and top boards by risk — refreshed every few minutes.
executiveDashboard.loadError: Could not load the executive dashboard.

okr.title: OKR progress (by objective)
okr.empty: No objectives for the current quarter with linked boards.

throughput.hint: Weekly completions use Copilot history. Without MongoDB or Copilot usage, values may stay at zero.

risk.title: Top 5 boards by risk (lowest risk index first)

portfolioAi.title: Multi-board portfolio assistant
portfolioAi.subtitle: Combines board metrics with OKRs and internal docs (RAG) for strategic Q&A.
portfolioAi.heatmapEmpty: No boards with cards to show.
portfolioAi.placeholder: e.g. Where are the biggest priority conflicts between teams?
portfolioAi.chip1: Which boards most threaten quarterly goals?
portfolioAi.chip2: Summarize risks across throughput and OKRs.
portfolioAi.chip3: Where should we focus in the next two weeks?

workspaceFluxy.intro: I’m the Flux-Board assistant. Here you’ll find shortcuts to key areas and a quick overview of what I can help with.
workspaceFluxy.boardHint: On an open board, I can discuss cards, metrics, and automations — use the Fluxy shortcut on the board for the full AI copilot.
workspaceFluxy.emptyChatLead: Ask about Flux-Board, navigation, or best practices. On an open board, Fluxy uses your cards as context.
workspaceFluxy.emptyChatLeadSprint: A sprint is in focus: I can answer using that sprint’s data (scope, burndown, and derived timeline).
workspaceFluxy.demoBody: On the Free plan there is a limit on workspace AI messages; open a board for the full contextual assistant.

sleeping.label: Resting
sleeping.desc: Ping me when you need me.

pointing.label: Look here

meta.title: Flux Board — A living board to ship with clarity.
meta.description: Priorities and context in one place: Kanban, Flux Goals, Flux Reports, Flux Forms, and AI (Fluxy, Copilot) per plan. Start free; 20-day Pro/Business trial, no credit card.
meta.jsonLdDescription: Visual work platform with Kanban, OKRs, reporting, public forms, and in-flow AI — for teams that want predictability from idea to delivery.

betaBanner.title: Paid subscriptions and checkout will be enabled in {days} days.
betaBanner.description: You are viewing a beta release. Capabilities, pricing presentation, and go-live timing may be refined before general availability. Early access helps us validate the product with teams like yours.

hero.chip: Visual flow · AI in the work · Goals · Reports · Forms

title.line1: The board where your team sees

hero.description: Cards with rich context, explicit priority, and reads ready for decisions. Flux Goals, Flux Reports, and Flux Forms in one place, with Fluxy and Copilot to speed up questions and next steps.

m3.label: Boards when you need to scale

why.heading: Less status chasing. More steady delivery.
why.description: When work only lives in chat, priorities blur. Here the board is the source of truth — with rich context and a visible cadence for everyone.

commercialPace.title: Kanban aligned with how you actually work
commercialPace.description: Columns and WIP that match your flow — triage, execution, or support. Everyone sees the same value queue.

insights.title: Context on the card — decisions where they belong
insights.description: Structured fields and daily insights cut meetings that exist only to “update status” and make the next move obvious.

executiveView.title: Reporting and portfolio without a shadow spreadsheet
executiveView.description: Roll up boards, export when you need proof, and show progress with numbers that match the work on the cards.

platform.heading: One platform to run the whole flow
platform.description: From intake to cycle close: reports, OKRs, forms, discovery, and alerts — in the surfaces your team already uses every day.

dailyInsights.name: Daily insights
dailyInsights.detail: See how your board is moving—keep cadence and catch bottlenecks early.

contextOnCards.name: Context on cards
contextOnCards.detail: Structured fields on each card so your team decides the same way, faster.

executiveBrief.name: Executive brief
executiveBrief.detail: Distill what leadership needs without rebuilding the story elsewhere.

portfolioAndMetrics.name: Portfolio and metrics
portfolioAndMetrics.detail: Roll-ups and exports when you need to show how delivery is really going.

fluxGoals.name: Flux Goals (OKRs)
fluxGoals.detail: Objectives and key results tied to the work on your boards—strategy and execution in one loop.

fluxForms.name: Flux Forms
fluxForms.detail: Public intake links that land structured requests straight on the right board—less inbox, fewer lost asks.

fluxReports.name: Flux Reports
fluxReports.detail: Executive dashboards and trends so leadership reads health and flow without rebuilding the story elsewhere.

discoveryAndDeals.name: Discovery and delivery
discoveryAndDeals.detail: From idea to done: hypotheses, requests, and work items share the same live context on the cards.

routinesAndAlerts.name: Routines and alerts
routinesAndAlerts.detail: Signals in the flow for throughput, WIP, and lead time so drift shows up before it surprises the retro.

how.heading: Three steps to a sustainable rhythm
how.description: Live in minutes — clear defaults help the team keep cadence week after week.

step1.title: Model the flow in one place
step1.text: Columns and limits that mirror how work arrives, moves, and finishes — everyone sees the same priority.

step2.title: Enrich every card
step2.text: Description, fields, and automated reads turn tasks into decisions anyone can pick up later.

step3.title: Scale with light governance
step3.text: Metrics, exports, and executive views grow with you without losing the thread of what is on the board.

cta.heading: Bring this board to your team today
cta.description: Start free to explore; unlock Pro or Business with a 20-day trial and no card on file. One home for work that should not get lost in the noise.

midCta.heading: Feel the flow on a board that tracks delivery
midCta.description: Create a board, bring the team, and keep decisions where the context lives — not scattered across threads and files.

trust.heading: Your data, your peace of mind
trust.intro: Built for teams that need predictability and control over where work and data live.
trust.bullet1: Encrypted traffic (HTTPS) and modern authentication (OAuth 2.0 + JWT) on the web app.
trust.bullet2: Multi-tenant by design: each organization keeps its boards and data isolated.
trust.bullet3: Documentation covers features, per-plan limits, and usage guidance.

roadmap.heading: What is shipping next — and which plan unlocks it
roadmap.description: A living roadmap: each item shows the capability and the tier that unlocks it.

sprint_engine.title: Sprint Engine and unified panel
sprint_engine.detail: Plan, run, and close sprints with a dedicated hub, forecasts, and backlog flow.

ceremonies.title: AI-assisted Scrum/Kanban ceremonies
ceremonies.detail: Planning, standup, review, and retro with board-context suggestions.

dependency_graph_visual.title: Visual dependency map
dependency_graph_visual.detail: Cross-board dependency mapping to reduce blockers and delays.

flux_docs_rag.title: Flux Docs + card context RAG
flux_docs_rag.detail: Knowledge base with semantic retrieval to enrich day-to-day decisions.

anomaly_email.title: Proactive anomalies and alerts
anomaly_email.detail: Deviation detection on throughput, WIP, and lead time with notifications and suggested actions.

spotlight.heading: AI that speeds decisions — without pulling you out of the flow
spotlight.description: Copilot, goals, and alerts work on board context to summarize, project, and surface what needs attention.

copilot.title: Board Copilot
copilot.body: Ask in natural language, request summaries, and spot bottlenecks without leaving the board — Fluxy uses card context to suggest next steps.
copilot.sampleQuestion: Which cards have been blocked for more than 3 days?
copilot.sampleAnswer: 3 blocked cards: "API Gateway" (5d), "Deploy Pipeline" (4d), "Schema Migration" (3d). Consider escalating with the tech lead.

okr.title: OKR Engine
okr.body: Objectives and key results linked to board cards. Track progress in real time with delivery outlook and drift alerts.

anomaly.title: Anomaly detection
anomaly.body: Watch throughput, WIP, and lead time with email and webhook alerts so you act before drift becomes a crisis.

stat4.label: boards when you need to scale

pricing.heading: Plans for every stage of growth
pricing.description: Start free and grow when you need AI, OKRs, and governance — clear per-seat pricing.
pricing.trialNote: 20-day trial on Pro and Business plans. No credit card required.
pricing.tierUnavailable: Not available in the catalog right now.

free.name: Free
free.desc: For teams getting started with organized workflows at no cost.

pro.name: Pro
pro.desc: For small teams that need AI, OKRs, and portfolio visibility.
pro.limits: Unlimited boards · Up to 10 members · 50 AI calls/day

business.name: Business
business.desc: For scaled operations: unlimited AI, full white-label, proactive alerts, and advanced governance.
business.limits: Unlimited boards · Up to 100 members · Unlimited AI

faq.heading: Frequently asked questions
faq.q1: What is included in the Free plan?
faq.a1: The Free plan gives you up to 3 Kanban boards, 1 user, 3 AI calls per day, public templates, and a 90-day activity log. Perfect for exploring the workflow before scaling up.
faq.q2: Can I try paid plans before subscribing?
faq.a2: Yes. Pro and Business include a 20-day trial with no credit card required. You get full access to all features of the chosen plan and only pay if you decide to continue.
faq.a3: Each active member of your organization counts as one seat. You choose how many seats you need and can adjust as your team grows. Annual billing saves 20% compared to monthly.
faq.q4: Can I upgrade or downgrade at any time?
faq.a4: Yes. Upgrades take effect immediately with prorated credit. Downgrades take effect at the end of the current billing cycle, with a 7-day grace period to adjust boards and users.
faq.a5: A conversational AI assistant embedded in your board. Ask questions in natural language about cards, request summaries, spot bottlenecks, and execute actions — all without leaving the Kanban view.
faq.q6: Can I export data and roll up multiple boards?
faq.a6: Yes. On higher tiers you get exports and portfolio views so you can track the full body of work without maintaining parallel spreadsheets.
faq.q7: How do AI limits work, and how is my data used?
faq.a7: Per-plan limits are shown in the pricing table and documentation. AI uses your workspace context to summarize and suggest — we do not use your data to train public models.

fluxyChat.title: Ask Fluxy
fluxyChat.subtitle: Straight answers from our FAQ
fluxyChat.subtitleLlm: Claude when configured — FAQ as fallback

fluxyChat.rateLimited: Too many messages from this network. Please try again in a little while.

login.subtitle: Sign in to see your team's flow with clarity.
login.sessionIssueGeneric: We couldn’t restore your session. Try signing in again, or contact your workspace administrator if this keeps happening.

sessionDiag.title: We couldn’t keep your session
sessionDiag.hint: Send the JSON (button below) to support so we can match it with server logs. It does not include your password, email, or cookies.
sessionDiag.copyHint: The support reference is not shown on this page; use the button only if support asks for the JSON.

kinds.no_cookies: No session cookies were sent to the server.
kinds.token_invalid: Session token was invalid or refresh failed.
kinds.user_not_found: No user record matched the current token.
kinds.client_timeout: Validation timed out in the browser (there may be no matching server log).
kinds.server_timeout: The server didn’t finish validation in time (slow MongoDB/network). Look for `[api/auth/session] validate wall timeout` in logs.

login.inviteBanner: You’re signing in with an invite. If you already have an account, use Sign in or Google/Microsoft — the invite applies to the matching email.

errors.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters.
errors.oauthPasswordOnly: This account uses social sign-in. Use Google or Microsoft.
errors.inviteInvalid: Invalid or expired invite, or it doesn’t match this account’s email.
errors.invitePlanLimit: This organization’s plan user limit has been reached.
errors.inviteConsumeFailed: The invite could not be applied. Try again.
errors.inviteOwnerConflict: Your account owns another organization. To accept the invite, use a different email or transfer ownership first.
errors.invitePlatformAdmin: This account cannot be linked via invite.
errors.inviteOAuthConflict: Could not link social sign-in. Try another provider or contact support.
errors.registerUseLoginForInvite: An account with this email already exists. Use Sign in (or Google/Microsoft) with the same email to accept the invite.

remember.register: Keep me connected after sign up

errors.generic: Sign-in with Google or Microsoft could not be completed. Try again.
errors.oauth_state: Session expired. Try signing in again.
errors.oauth_profile: Could not read your profile from the provider.
errors.oauth_exchange: Could not complete sign-in with the provider.
errors.oauth_email_unverified: Your email is not verified with the provider.
errors.oauth_no_email: No email was returned for this account.
errors.oauth_account_conflict: This email is already linked to another account for this provider.
errors.oauth_invite_owner_conflict: Your account owns another organization. Use a different email or transfer ownership to accept the invite.
errors.oauth_invite_platform_admin: This account cannot be linked via invite.
errors.oauth_plan_limit: Organization user limit reached for this plan.
errors.oauth_consume_failed: The invite could not be applied. Try again.
errors.oauth_not_configured: Social sign-in is not configured on the server.
errors.rate_limited: Too many attempts. Wait and try again.

inviteJoined.title: Welcome to the organization
inviteJoined.description: Your invite was accepted. You’re now part of this team workspace.

orgAudit.title: Organization activity
orgAudit.subtitle: Recent audit events for your active organization.
orgAudit.mongoDisabled: Audit storage is not fully configured. Events may not be recorded in this environment.

onboarding.fluxyHint: I’m Fluxy — once your board is ready, I’ll be there to answer questions and suggest shortcuts.

fluxyHero.title: Great to see you here!
fluxyHero.body: I’m Fluxy, Flux-Board’s AI assistant. I’m here to help you organize work, answer board questions, and suggest shortcuts when you need them.
fluxyHero.intro: Let’s set up your first board in a few steps — you can customize everything later.

descriptions.step1: Choose a template and a name. You can skip if you want.
descriptions.step2: Drag the columns to adjust the flow. You can skip.
descriptions.step3: One card is enough to get started. You can also skip.

errors.initTimeout: Loading your boards took too long. Check your connection and try again.
errors.needSession: We could not confirm your session. Refresh the page or sign in again.

placeholders.cardDescriptionOptional: What needs to happen? (keep it short)

preview.title: Template preview
preview.description: When you continue, we'll create your board with these columns and you can reorder in the next step.

step1.preparing: Checking your account and boards… The buttons will enable in a moment.
step1.sessionInvalid: We couldn’t validate your session (server or database may be unavailable). Fix the environment or sign in again.

step2.reorderDescription: Your cards will be created using the order/columns from this template.

step3.title: First card
step3.description: Create a simple item to get started. You can skip and go straight to the board.

boards.pageSubtitle: Search, filter, and open your boards. Metrics and portfolio overview are below when you need them.

filters.searchPlaceholder: Search by name or board ID...

empty.noBoards: No boards yet. Click "{newBoardName}" to create.
empty.noResults: No boards found for the applied filters.

modal.methodologyHint: Scrum: sprints, product goal, and sprint ceremonies. Kanban: continuous flow, WIP, and service cadences. Lean Six Sigma: DMAIC project flow (Define–Measure–Analyze–Improve–Control) with artifact assistant.

capabilityStrip.title: Product map
capabilityStrip.subtitle: Where each capability lives — most of them are inside a board.

triage.name: Smart Triage
triage.hint: Flux Forms: public form that creates cards and classifies with board context.

copilot.name: Central AI
copilot.hint: Board copilot: chat with tools over the Kanban (panel on screen).

timeline.name: Timeline
timeline.hint: On the board, switch to Timeline view (due dates on a timeline).

portal.name: Client portal
portal.hint: Client portal in the header: read-only link for external stakeholders.

automations.name: Flux Automations
automations.hint: Automations modal: triggers and actions on cards and columns.

reports.name: Flux Reports
reports.hint: Reports in the menu: org-wide dashboard and AI narrative per chart.

anomalyAlerts.title: Anomaly notifications
anomalyAlerts.hint: Configure email when the monitor flags risks on this board (minimum severity, types, extra recipients). Org-wide alerts use org admin emails or environment variables.
anomalyAlerts.emailEnabled: Send email for alerts on this board
anomalyAlerts.kindsHint: Empty = all types. Uncheck to restrict.
anomalyAlerts.recipientsHint: Optional. If empty, we use organization administrators.

automations.title: Flux Automations — internal rules
automations.hint: Pick a trigger and an action. Time-based rules (stuck, due date, completion %) are evaluated when the automation cron runs (daily on Vercel Hobby; upgrade to Pro for per-minute scheduling).

triggers.card_stuck_in_column: Card stuck in column for N days

automations.nlHint: Write in Portuguese or English. AI maps your text to Flux’s technical format using this board’s columns.
automations.nlPlaceholder: E.g. when a card stays more than 5 days in Proposal, notify the owner and set priority to Urgent.
automations.nlAddRule: Add this rule to the list
automations.nlTooShort: Write at least one full sentence.
automations.nlApplied: Rule added to the list (save to persist).

toasts.saveError: Failed to save changes. Check your connection and try again.

collab.pollingHint: Real-time unavailable; the board refreshes periodically.

copilotNudges.coachSubtitle: Based on limits, workload, and blockers on this board.

fluxyWelcome.title: Hi! I’m Fluxy
fluxyWelcome.body: I’ll walk you through this board in a few steps: columns, cards, the Fluxy assistant, and useful shortcuts. When you’re ready, we’ll start the tour.

fluxyCelebrate.title: You did it!
fluxyCelebrate.body: You’ve finished the guided tour — you’re ready to move work forward with confidence. Fluxy is here whenever you need a hand on this board.

header.title: Board name and client
header.body: The title identifies this board. When set, the client label appears below — useful for portfolio and the portal.

column.title: Columns are stages
column.body: Each column is a workflow stage (e.g. Backlog → In progress). Drag the column header to reorder.

newCard.title: New card
newCard.body: Use + on the column to create a card in that stage. The card inherits the column where it was created.

card.title: Drag between columns
card.body: Drag a card to another column to change its stage. You can also reorder within a column by dragging.

copilot.title: Fluxy — board assistant
copilot.body: On desktop, open it from the tools rail on the right (bottom corner). On mobile, use the board’s floating tools menu. Fluxy answers with this board’s context.

daily.title: Daily Insights
daily.body: Generate summaries and actions from transcripts or notes — AI organizes context and suggests cards.

reports.title: Reports
reports.body: In Reports you see org-wide metrics, charts, and narrative about performance.

executiveBrief.title: Executive Brief (AI)
executiveBrief.error: Could not generate the executive brief.

portal.title: Client portal — external visibility
portal.hint: Share project status without granting access to the internal board. The portal is read-only.
portal.columnsHint: All selected = show every column that has items.
portal.cardIdsHint: One ID per line. If empty, all cards from the columns above are included.

fluxIntelligence.title: Flux Intelligence
fluxIntelligence.insightsHint: Local signals (no real-time AI cost). Best for daily team review.
fluxIntelligence.depsHint: Map from card `blockedBy` links — red: active chain; green: blocker done.

backlogPrioritize.title: Backlog priority (Fluxy)
backlogPrioritize.empty: Click compute. Cards in the first bucket (backlog) are ranked.

intakeVision.hint: Upload a screenshot or mock; AI suggests a title and fills notes. Requires a vision model configured on the server.

intakeVoice.hint: Speak, paste text, or upload audio; Fluxy drafts title and description (same card-context feature).
intakeVoice.error: Could not generate the card right now.
intakeVoice.transcribeSuccess: Audio transcribed — review and tap Generate card if you want.
intakeVoice.voiceNotSupported: Speech recognition is not supported in this browser.
intakeVoice.voiceMicError: Microphone error. Check permissions and try again.

smartEnrich.busy: Generating suggestions from your title and board…
smartEnrich.error: Could not fetch suggestions right now.
smartEnrich.dueSimilar: Based on average lead time from {count} similar completed card(s).
smartEnrich.dueNone: No similar completed cards; adjust the date manually if needed.

duplicate.mergeToast: Content merged into the existing card.

scrum.title: Scrum — estimate and sprint
scrum.description: Fibonacci story points for velocity; include this card in planning or active sprints.

kanban.title: Kanban — service class
kanban.description: Choose how this item is treated in the flow (classes of service). Column WIP is shown as context.

methodology.sprintNeedSave: Save the card with a real ID before linking it to a sprint.
methodology.noSprints: No sprints yet — create one in the Sprint panel.
methodology.serviceClassUnset: Not set (use heuristics on the card)

toasts.missingTitleAndDescription: Please provide a title and a description to generate context.

logs.contextTimeout: Timed out while generating AI context.
logs.unableToGenerateContext: Unable to generate AI context. Adjust the title/description and try again.

description.edit: Adjust identification, content, and status — changes reflect on the board right away.
description.new: Set the essentials now; you can refine title, description, and links later.

identification.title: Identification and column
identification.description: Unique ID and current position in the board flow.

content.title: Main content
content.description: Title on the board and description in blocks — use the AI button when you want suggestions based on what you already wrote.

statusDue.title: Status and due date
statusDue.description: Priority, work stage, and target completion date.

dependencies.title: Dependencies
dependencies.description: Cards that must be done before this one (blocked by).

dor.title: Refinement (Definition of Ready)
dor.description: Check what is ready before pulling this item into a sprint.

dod.title: Definition of Done
dod.description: Board-level checklist — required to complete when enforcement is on.

refinementReadiness.title: Refinement readiness
refinementReadiness.description: Heuristic 0–100 score: description, acceptance, estimate, dependencies, and history. Complements AI refinement below.
refinementReadiness.label: Readiness score
refinementReadiness.hintMid: Good progress — a few gaps remain.

refineAi.title: Backlog refinement (AI)
refineAi.description: Suggested acceptance criteria, risks, and dependencies — review before adopting.
refineAi.empty: Add a title and description first.

unblock.title: AI unblock assist
unblock.description: Suggested next steps to resolve dependencies (uses your org AI limits).

labels.title: Labels
labels.description: Select existing tags or create new ones for this board.

links.title: Links
links.empty: No links. Click "Add link" to include one.

assignee.label: Assignee
assignee.placeholder: Search member by name or ID…
assignee.hint: Pick a board member without typing their ID.

blockedBy.label: Blocked by
blockedBy.hint: Select predecessor cards. Links appear on the Timeline.

aria.dragHandle: Drag to move the modal — double-click to re-center

tooltips.trigger: Generate AI context (based on Title and Description)

log.title: AI connectivity log
log.emptyMessage: The log will appear here once generation starts.

result.autoFilledText: The Title and Description were filled automatically.

depsTab.title: Cross-board dependencies
depsTab.description: Link this card to cards on other boards in the same organization.
depsTab.hintKinds: depends_on: this card depends on the other (the other is the blocker) · blocks: this card blocks the other · related_to: related without a blocking role.

kind.depends_on: This card depends on the other

depsTab.suggestionsHint: Pairs with similarity ≥0.85 on title/description (daily job with embeddings). Accept creates a “related” link with the shown confidence.
depsTab.needSave: Save the card before managing cross-board dependencies.

docRefs.title: Linked documents
docRefs.description: Attach knowledge-base docs for card context.

history.title: Movement and automations
history.description: Data stored on the card about the column and recent automation runs.
history.noEnteredAt: No recorded date for entering this column.

aiTab.title: AI context
aiTab.description: Generate title, description, and enrichment from what you already wrote.
aiTab.hint: Use when the card already has a title and description. The panel shows progress and results.
aiTab.needTitleDesc: Fill in title and description on the Details tab to enable generation.
aiTab.epicSectionHint: Break the epic into backlog cards or generate an onboarding brief for the assignee (requires a plan with AI).
aiTab.needSavedCard: Save the card first — decomposition only works for cards that already exist on the board.
aiTab.needAssignee: Set an assignee on the Details tab to generate the briefing.
aiTab.decomposeSuccess: Epic decomposed — the board was refreshed.
aiTab.briefingSuccess: Assignee briefing saved on the card.

completeMove.tooltip: Move to column or mark complete
completeMove.menuTitle: Which column should this move to?

quickActions.pinToTopTooltip: Move to top of column (backlog order)

sprintMenu.title: Sprint
sprintMenu.empty: No planning or active sprint. Open the Sprint panel.

column.tourEmptyCardHint: No cards yet — use + above to create the first one.
column.emptyHint: No cards — drop cards here or add a new one

flowInsights.blockedTooltip: Open cards with a blocking dependency in this column: {count}.
flowInsights.overdueTooltip: Open cards past their due date in this column: {count}.

tooltips.wipExceeded: Over this column’s WIP limit — reduce WIP or move work out (Kanban).

activity.title: Activity
activity.subtitle: Change history for this board
activity.noMongo: Activity history requires a persistent database configured on the server.

executionInsights.title: Execution & focus
executionInsights.subtitle: Next best actions and WIP risk by column
executionInsights.emptyNextActions: No open cards to prioritize right now.

addColumnModal.wipPlaceholder: e.g. 5 — leave empty for no limit
addColumnModal.policyLabel: Column policy (how we use this column)
addColumnModal.wipInvalid: WIP must be a whole number between 1 and 999.

title.replace: Import {count} cards? Replaces the current ones.

description.replace: Confirm to replace the board's current content.
description.merge: It will update {existingCount} existing card(s) and add {newCount}.

dailyDelete.title: Delete this Daily AI history summary?

serpro.name: SERPRO (Federal Revenue)
serpro.description: Validation of tax status and the CNPJ with the Federal Revenue Service, through a certified partner, ensuring compliance and the integrity of the registration for business decisions.

susep.name: SUSEP
susep.description: Lookup of public authorization and professional standing records (regulatory body), ensuring only qualified partners operate in sensitive workflows.

serasa.name: SERASA
serasa.description: Credit analysis and financial indicators via a specialized partner, supporting due diligence, access provisioning, and risk decisions on registrations.

executiveView.title: Production Map — {appName}
executiveView.description: A consolidated view of what's already in production: business lines and integrated validations (SERPRO, SUSEP, SERASA) performed in each workflow.

integrations.title: Integrated validations
integrations.description: Automated checks performed by the platform to ensure regulatory compliance and data integrity.

inProduction.title: Records in Production
inProduction.description: Roles by team and business line, with the applied validations.

entry.description: Paste the daily transcript (or attach a .txt/.md file) to generate a practical view of the next steps.

generating.title: Generation in progress. Follow the Status tab.
generating.description: You can close and reopen this modal without losing the current progress.

transcribing.title: Transcribing audio with Whisper…
transcribing.description: Your file is sent securely for transcription. Review and edit the text below before generating the summary.

entry.transcriptPreviewHint: Edit the text if needed before generating insights and cards.
entry.transcriptPlaceholder: Ex: yesterday we finished... today we're going to... blocker on...

voiceToBoard.title: Voice → board (detected cards)
voiceToBoard.hint: Heuristic match between transcript and card titles. Open a card to update status or blockers.

log.title: AI connectivity log
log.emptyMessage: The log will appear here once generation starts.

filters.searchPlaceholder: Search in summary, context, and lists...

tooltips.rewritten: Content rewritten and structured by AI from the transcript.

card.noneYet: No cards created for this summary yet.

history.emptyNoMatches: No summary found with the provided filters.
history.emptyNoSummaryYet: There is no saved summary for this board yet.

error.noCreateItems: No items in "Create" to turn into cards.

both.title: {count} card(s) created successfully.
both.description: {existingCount} item(s) already existed on the board and were only flagged.

none.title: No new cards created.
none.description: All suggested items already exist on the board.

warning.noClipboardSupport: Your browser does not support automatic copy.

error.noTranscript: Provide or attach the daily transcript.
error.title: Error while generating the summary with AI.

generate.timeout: Timed out while generating the Daily AI.

transcribe.done: Transcription received. Review the text below.

success.heuristic: Summary structured without effective AI usage (heuristic mode).

error.timeout: Timed out while generating the Daily AI.
error.error: Error while generating summary with AI.

timeline.viewTableTooltip: Table (T): sort and edit in rows
timeline.emptyTitle: No cards with a due date
timeline.emptyHint: Set a completion date on cards to see them here. Board filters apply to this view as well.
timeline.barAria: Due date for {title}: {date}. Drag to change or use arrow keys.

table.emptyHint: Adjust filters or search — the same rules as Kanban apply here.

filters.title: Filters
filters.sprintFilterHint: Narrows the board to cards in the active sprint.

productGoalStrip.label: Product goal
productGoalStrip.emptyGoal: No product goal yet — set it in Scrum & DoD settings.

scrumSettings.title: Scrum on Kanban — product goal & Definition of Done
scrumSettings.titleAgile: Agile methodology — Product Goal & Definition of Done
scrumSettings.methodologyHint: Scrum uses sprints and events (planning, daily, review, retro). Kanban emphasizes continuous flow, WIP limits, and review cadences. Lean Six Sigma structures improvement as DMAIC. Leaving Scrum for Kanban or LSS is blocked while a sprint is active.
scrumSettings.productGoalPlaceholder: What outcome does this board pursue for the client or product?
scrumSettings.backlogHint: Cards in this column are ordered top-to-bottom as backlog priority (drag to rank).
scrumSettings.dodEnabled: Enable Definition of Done checklist on cards
scrumSettings.dodEnforce: Block moving to a “done” column or marking complete until all items are checked
scrumSettings.dodEnforceHint: Turn on to enforce the checklist on the server and in drag-and-drop — not only in the card editor.
scrumSettings.wipEnforcementStrict: Strict — moves that exceed a column limit require a one-time justification
scrumSettings.wipEnforcementSoft: Soft — limits are advisory; moves are never blocked by count
scrumSettings.dodPlaceholder:
Tests passed
Documentation updated
Stakeholder sign-off
scrumSettings.doneColumnsHint: Leave all unchecked to auto-detect from column names (e.g. Concluído, Done, Produção).

wipOverride.title: Over WIP limit
wipOverride.body: This move exceeds the column WIP limit. Enter a short justification to continue — it is sent once with your next save.
wipOverride.reasonHint: Minimum 8 characters. Tip: set WIP to “soft” in Agile methodology settings if limits should stay advisory only.

checklist.define: Project charter, measurable problem, scope, and stakeholders.
checklist.measure: Measurement plan, baseline, and operational definition of Y.
checklist.control: Control plan, standard work, and reaction to deviations.

lssAssist.title: Lean Six Sigma assistant
lssAssist.contextPlaceholder: Describe the process, problem, or available data…
lssAssist.premiumLocked: Upgrade to Business to unlock steering narrative, FMEA, executive hypothesis framing, control-chart interpretation, and audit readiness checklists.

incrementReview.title: Increment — what was delivered
incrementReview.empty: No completed cards in this view.

fluxyCopilot.salaFooterHint: Use the composer in the Fluxy room tab above for messages and notifications.
fluxyCopilot.salaSummaryEmpty: Board Fluxy room is ready for handoffs.
fluxyCopilot.salaQuickDesc: Enter a card ID below for board-room actions. Fluxy mentions and policies use that context.
fluxyCopilot.salaComposerPlaceholder: Post to Fluxy room… (@name, /block, notify the assignee)
fluxyCopilot.notifyPipelineHint: With Fluxy on, phrases like “notify the assignee” may trigger push notifications to inferred recipients (with confirmation when needed).
fluxyCopilot.notifyConfirmBody: Fluxy will notify the listed people. Send?
fluxyCopilot.needCardIdForCtas: Enter the card ID above to use the buttons on this message.
fluxyCopilot.suggestionNotifyAssignee: Open Fluxy room and ask to notify the assignee
fluxyCopilot.suggestionNotifyBlocked: Notify assignee about the block (Fluxy room)
fluxyCopilot.title: Fluxy
fluxyCopilot.subtitle: Board assistant
fluxyCopilot.emptyIntro: Ask about the board, request summaries or actions. Ideas below:
fluxyCopilot.emptyTipNlq: Structured search: use /query (e.g. «/query urgent cards with no owner»).
fluxyCopilot.emptyTipVoice: Use the mic or ask to move cards and change priority.
fluxyCopilot.emptyTipWeekly: Try: “Which cards have been idle for more than 5 days?” or a weekly brief.
fluxyCopilot.insightBlocked: {count} blocked — consider notifying assignees via the Fluxy room.
fluxyCopilot.demoBody: Upgrade to Pro/Business for unlimited Fluxy and AI automations.
fluxyCopilot.placeholder: Ask a question or request an action…
fluxyCopilot.hintFooter: Tip: use voice or commands like “move card X to In progress”.
fluxyCopilot.voiceNotSupported: Your browser does not support speech recognition (Web Speech API).
fluxyCopilot.voiceMicError: Could not capture audio. Check the microphone and try again.

sleeping.label: Resting
sleeping.desc: Ping me when you need me.

pointing.label: Look here
pointing.desc: Check the spot I’m pointing to.

fluxyDock.openAssistant: Open Fluxy — questions about this board
fluxyDock.restoreAria: Show Fluxy assistant shortcut on the board

desktopToolsRail.handleTitle: Hover to show board tools, or click to pin the strip open

nlq.label: Smart board query
nlq.placeholder: e.g. urgent cards with no owner · throughput last week
nlq.aria: Natural language query about this board
nlq.cleared: NLQ filter and metric panel cleared.
nlq.hint: In Copilot, use /query plus your question. Recent queries appear in the list while typing.

intelligence.workloadHint: AI analysis of card distribution across team members with rebalancing suggestions.

detailChrome.expandAria: Show product goal, sprint bar and matrix weight filters
detailChrome.collapse: Collapse product context and matrix filters

flowHealth.title: Flow health
flowHealth.wipOk: All columns respect configured WIP limits.
flowHealth.wipBreached: At least one column exceeds its WIP limit.
flowHealth.cfdHint: Org-wide CFD lives in Flux Reports (paid / portfolio).
flowHealth.cfdGated: CFD history needs portfolio access or cron snapshots.

knowledgeGraph.title: Knowledge Graph
knowledgeGraph.empty: Not enough data to generate graph.

wipRisk.title: WIP risk by column
wipRisk.emptyMessage: No critical bottlenecks (>= {minItems} items in progress).

screenReaderInstructions.draggable: Drag and drop cards using the keyboard. Use Tab to focus a card. Press Enter/Space to start dragging. Use the arrow keys to switch between columns/positions, then press Enter/Space to drop.

cardTemplates.noTemplatesHint: Save a card as template to reuse it later

myWork.title: My Work
myWork.empty: No cards assigned to you right now.

deliveryForecast.title: Delivery Forecast
deliveryForecast.monteNarrative: Use p50 as the median outlook; p85 ≈ high confidence to finish open scope; p95 when you need extra buffer (e.g. compliance or fixed dates).
deliveryForecast.whatIfHint: Adjust scope and capacity; optionally describe in natural language.
deliveryForecast.nlPlaceholder: e.g. remove 3 items and 20% more capacity

fluxAiHub.title: AI in Flux
fluxAiHub.subtitle: Shortcuts to intelligence features: portfolio, docs, forecasting, Copilot, sprint cockpit, and OKRs.

governance.title: Governance & agents
governance.body: Business tier: configurable autonomy levels, audit trail for agent runs, and MCP hub on the roadmap. Daily digest e-mails use per-user idempotency per day.

docs.title: Flux Docs
docs.desc: Document base used by Copilot and the portfolio assistant.

forecast.title: Reports and scenarios
forecast.desc: Monte Carlo, throughput, and what-if on Flux Reports.

copilot.title: Copilot
copilot.desc: Open a board and use Copilot from the side panel.

sprintCockpit.title: Sprint cockpit
sprintCockpit.desc: Guided checklist for planning, daily flow, review, and retro across your boards.

okrs.title: Flux Goals (OKRs)
okrs.desc: Connect execution to key results; future auto-prioritization uses this link.

sprintCockpit.title: Sprint cockpit
sprintCockpit.subtitle: A lightweight guide to run the Scrum loop with the tools you already have in Flux-Board.
sprintCockpit.footer: Run planning and ceremonies from each board’s sprint tools; this page keeps the sequence visible for the team.

planning.title: Sprint planning
planning.desc: Use board backlog + sprint planning AI to size and pull the right scope.

daily.title: Daily flow
daily.desc: Daily AI, voice transcription, and WIP coach nudges on the live board.

review.title: Review
review.desc: Use increment review and delivery forecast to demo what actually shipped.

retro.title: Retro
retro.desc: Capture insights from sentiment and flow metrics; track experiments as Kaizen cards.

docsPage.title: Flux Docs

empty.title: No document selected
empty.subtitle: Pick a document from the tree on the left or create a new one to get started.
empty.step1: Use New or + subdoc in the sidebar to create documents and hierarchy.
empty.step2: Edit the title and body; content autosaves after you pause typing.
empty.step3: Open Generate with AI above to draft from boards, dailies, or OKRs.

hint.title: How to use Flux Docs
hint.step1: Organize documents in the left tree (root and subdocuments).
hint.step2: Use search above to find titles and snippets quickly.
hint.step3: Use the Edit tab for the rich toolbar; Preview renders your Markdown.
hint.step4: Import .md files or export the current doc; Save now pushes immediately.

aiBanner.body: Tip: use “Generate with AI” at the top of this page to create reports from your board and save them as a document.

docsPage.importConfirm: Replace all current content with the imported file?
docsPage.editorPlaceholder: Start writing… Use the toolbar for headings, lists, tables, and links.

cardBlock.expandToEdit: Expand this section to load the rich editor.

specPlanPage.title: Scope planning from document
specPlanPage.upgradeBody: Upgrade your plan to import specifications and generate board cards with AI aligned to Scrum, Kanban, or Lean Six Sigma.
specPlanPage.privacyNote: Document text is sent to the AI provider for analysis. Review cards before applying.
specPlanPage.methodHintScrum: Stories, acceptance criteria, and story points.
specPlanPage.methodHintKanban: Value flow, service classes, and small items.
specPlanPage.persistenceLoading: Still checking whether background history is available for this board. Wait a moment and try again.
specPlanPage.streamInterruptWarning: If you leave or close this tab, the in-page run may stop. Use the main button for a background run that continues after you leave.
specPlanPage.secondRunModalBody: There is already an analysis in progress for this board. Starting another may use additional quota. Continue anyway?
specPlanPage.secondRunConflictServer: A run is already in progress for this board.
specPlanPage.activeRunBannerTitle: You have analysis in progress — select the board to resume
specPlanPage.acceptCheck: I reviewed the suggestions and confirm I can apply them to this board.
specPlanPage.noInputError: Upload a file or paste text.
specPlanPage.errorOpenPanelHint: Open the panel for the full log and technical details.
specPlanPage.privacyHistoryNote: Runs may be stored in this board's history (requires MongoDB). You can return later and resume.

sticky.analyzingBackground: Background analysis — you can leave this page.
sticky.cancelRequested: Cancellation requested — waiting for the server to stop the run.
sticky.runSyncFailed: Could not sync this run (network or wrong board). Confirm the board or refresh the page.

specPlanPage.cancelBackgroundTimeout: The cancel request timed out. Check your connection and try again.
specPlanPage.cancelBackgroundError: Could not request cancellation. Try again.
specPlanPage.backgroundRequiresMongo: Background runs and history need a persistent database on the server.
specPlanPage.reviewEmpty: No cards yet. Start an analysis or restore a run from history.
specPlanPage.historyEmpty: No saved runs for this board.

stepperFriendly.parse: We read your PDF, DOCX, or pasted text so the AI can work from the real content.
stepperFriendly.chunks: Text is split into chunks for embeddings and to find the most relevant excerpts.
stepperFriendly.embeddings: Chunks are turned into vectors for semantic understanding.
stepperFriendly.retrieval: We pick excerpts that best match questions about requirements, risks, and scope.
stepperFriendly.outline: The AI structures sections and key requirements before suggesting work.
stepperFriendly.work: We draft work items aligned with your methodology. Long PDFs can take several minutes here—the run is still in progress.
stepperFriendly.cards: We map everything to your board columns with suggested titles, priorities, and tags.

analysisModal.title: Analysis progress
analysisModal.subtitle: Document read, semantic retrieval, and AI phases
analysisModal.logEmpty: The log fills in as analysis events arrive.

logEvents.chunksReady: Text split into {count} chunks (avg size {avgSize} chars; subsampled: {truncated}).
logEvents.embeddingsFailed: Embedding call failed — using truncated excerpt for outline.
logEvents.workItemsLlmStarted: Generating work items (~{chars} chars of outline in prompt). Large PDFs may take several minutes at this step.
logEvents.cardsLlmStarted: Mapping to board cards: AI in progress ({count} work items, ~{chars} chars in prompt). Large specs may take a while at this step.

releases.title: Releases
releases.subtitle: Manage versions, changelogs and deploys linked to sprints and cards.
releases.emptyDescription: Create your first release from a closed sprint — Flux suggests the version and generates the changelog with AI.
releases.selectHint: Pick a release on the left to see details, notes and timeline.
releases.upgradeDescription: The releases module shares the Sprint Engine feature. Upgrade to enable.

form.subtitle: Version can be auto-suggested from the latest closed sprint.
